name: Saren-Volyn — Crystalline Lattice
description: Three corridors through the lattice. West: long and covered. Centre: short and blind — interference kills sensors. East: direct and contested. The bunker and the relay anchor opposite ends.
ambientRegister: Prismatic, fractured sightlines. Crystalline resonance, comms static in the interference.
designRationale: Corridor map without one-tiling: every lattice gap is 3 wide, every destination has two doors (bunker: front + rear; relay: two ramps). The interference field makes the central corridor a risk-trade — fastest line, no sensors. Three missions use the same terrain from three different directions.

grid.implementation: Fill defaultTile. Apply regions in order (later wins). Apply tileOverrides. Regions flagged interference reduce vision by 2 inside the zone.

tileOverrides.label: Relay supply node — defender eco at the S ramp
C-corridor cache — attacker eco, seats t3 from the S edge

keyFeatures.label: Asset Bunker
keyFeatures.description: front door N + rear exit W

keyFeatures.label: North Relay
keyFeatures.description: two ramps — can't be one-tiled shut

syndicate_m2.objectiveLabel: Quiet insertion from the southwest. The bunker's front door is watched; the rear exit isn't — they don't know you know about it. Don't leave a nameable body.

conditionalElements.element: Three lines in: south-edge hug to the rear exit, central corridor under interference (their sensors are blind too), east corridor if it goes loud.
Asset sits in your custody for hours after this — that gap is the story.

player.designNote: Speed and Reposition — in and out of the rear exit
Covers the exfil from the SW spur
Seals the rear exit behind you

enemy.designNote: Interior fire down the entry hall
Patrol — Far Sight cuts through refraction

economyNotes.player: No extractors either side — every body is accountable. (You were told not to leave a nameable one.)
economyNotes.enemy: Fixed garrison. What you slip past stays slipped past.

pirates_m2.objectiveLabel: Hit from the east edge, fast, before a real response spools up. The buyer's unknown sender pings the terminal mid-mission — be standing there when it does.

conditionalElements.element: Covenant response forms at the relay and comes down the S ramp — the reinforcement vector is the map's other end.

player.designNote: Fast in from the east edge before response spools
Cuts the bunker shell if the door jams

reinforcements.designNote: Response forms at the relay, comes down the S ramp

economyNotes.player: No Siphon — the response clock outruns any economy you could seat.
economyNotes.enemy: Priests are fixed; the t4 column is their sustain. Be gone before it arrives.

syndicate_m3.objectiveLabel: Push north up all three corridors and ring the relay. The platform has two ramps — take both or hold neither. The surface fight is cover for work inside.

conditionalElements.element: Relay-activity + Soldan scenes gate on turn & consequence score.
Covenant reinforcement enters the east corridor turn 4 — the route you didn't take stays dangerous.

player.designNote: Seats on the C-corridor cache — funds the sustained push
Corridor swings — west and east at once
Overwatch up the blind central line
Rings the relay — the actual objective

enemy.designNote: The line, dug into the interference
Strips your tempo at the gaps
The relay's own supply — their rebuy point, behind the walls
East spur — counters your blind corridor

reinforcements.designNote: Column up the corridor you didn't take

economyNotes.player: Assessor seats the relocated C-corridor cache by t3 — ≈1 rebuy per 3 turns sustains the push up the corridors.
economyNotes.enemy: The relay runs its own Seeker on the supply node at the S ramp — regrowing from t2. Ring the relay; a rout is a treadmill.
